name: ambientROOM (Ambient Displays)
short_description: An augmented room that conveys digital information at the periphery of awareness through ambient light, shadow, sound, airflow, and water movement.
long_description: ambientROOM is a Tangible Media Group prototype that turns architectural space into a slow, calm interface, using subtle ambient displays such as patterns of light cast by a water tank, sound, airflow, and physical handles like bottles and a clock to keep people aware of background information without demanding foreground attention. Introduced as part of Ishii and Ullmer's Tangible Bits vision, it is a foundational example of ambient/peripheral displays and calm technology, framing information delivery as an environmental, embodied experience rather than a screen-based one. It matters to physical AI and interaction design as an early articulation of how computation can recede into the periphery of a physical space and communicate through material change rather than explicit notification.
ai_note: ambientROOM is a tangible/ambient display technique mapping data to light, sound, airflow, and water movement; no AI or machine learning runs in the system.

images.caption: The ambientROOM prototype, in which a water tank projects rippling caustic light patterns onto surfaces as an ambient display of background information.
images.alt_text: A small augmented office space with shifting patterns of rippling light projected onto a wall and ceiling, alongside everyday physical objects used as controls.

sources.title: Ambient Displays: Turning Architectural Space into an Interface between People and Digital Information
ambientROOM
sources.publisher: MIT Media Lab, Tangible Media Group
